para.text: Pursuant to Transportation Article, §13-618, Annotated Code of Maryland, the fees in §B of this regulation shall be distributed equally between the Motor Vehicle Administration and the Chesapeake Bay Trust.
Chesapeake Bay registration plate fees is as follows:
For original Chesapeake Bay registration plates, the fee as set in COMAR 11.11.05.04(A)(3)(a); and
For the renewal of Chesapeake Bay registration plates, the fee as set in COMAR 11.11.05.04(A)(3)(b).
Money collected through the sale of Chesapeake Bay Commemorative Plates and due to the Chesapeake Bay Trust shall be transferred monthly to the Trust.

para.text: The fee provided for in COMAR 11.11.05.04(A)(3)(a) for original special agriculture registration plates shall be distributed equally between the Motor Vehicle Administration and the Maryland Agricultural Education Foundation.
The fee provided for in COMAR 11.11.05.04(A)(3)(b) for renewal of special agriculture registration plates shall be distributed to the Maryland Agricultural Education Foundation.
Money collected through the sale and renewal of special agriculture registration plates and due to the Maryland Agricultural Education Foundation shall be transferred monthly to the Foundation.

para.text: The Program covers all medically necessary care, including all health care services to identify and correct physical and mental problems that are covered in the State Plan, or that are allowable under the federal Medicaid program as described in §1905(a) of the Social Security Act.
For a foster care child, the Program also covers upon entry to or moving within the foster care system:
A brief initial check-up; and
A comprehensive EPSDT screen as described in §C of this regulation.
The Program covers EPSDT comprehensive well-child services in accordance with the EPSDT periodicity schedule, which includes the following:
EPSDT screening services which comprise the following:
A comprehensive health and developmental history, including assessment of both physical and mental health and development;
Age-appropriate immunizations;
Age and risk appropriate laboratory tests, including blood lead levels that are required at specific ages regardless of risk;
Comprehensive unclothed physical examination; and
Health education and anticipatory guidance;
Vision services that comprise:
Vision screening delivered by the EPSDT screening provider according to the EPSDT periodicity schedule;
Vision screening and vision services delivered by optometrists and opticians, including eyeglasses, as described in COMAR 10.09.14.04;
Hearing services that comprise:
Hearing screening delivered by the EPSDT screening provider according to the EPSDT periodicity schedule; and
Hearing screening and hearing aid services, as described in COMAR 10.09.51.04; and
Dental services, including:
Oral health assessment by the EPSDT screening provider and referral to a dentist; and
Dental services, as specified in COMAR 10.09.05.04.
Additional Medically Necessary Plan of Treatment Services.
The Program also covers the EPSDT treatment services listed in §D(2) of this regulation when the services are:
Necessary to identify, correct, or ameliorate defects and physical and mental illnesses and conditions;
Rendered in accordance with accepted professional standards; and
Delivered in accordance with a plan of treatment.
EPSDT services covered under §D of this regulation include:
Chiropractic services;
Mental health services or behavioral health services, or both, when the diagnosis of a participant is not included under the specialty mental health system, as described in COMAR 10.67.08.02;
Nutritional counseling services;
Occupational therapy services;
Speech therapy services;
Medically monitored intensive inpatient treatment services provided in an intermediate care facility, as specified in COMAR 10.47.02.09 or COMAR 10.63.03.14, whichever is applicable;
Clinically managed high intensity residential treatment services provided in an intermediate care facility, as specified in COMAR 10.47.02.09 or COMAR 10.63.03.13, whichever is applicable;
Environmental lead investigations, as specified in COMAR 26.16.02.04 and .05 when there is a confirmed elevated blood lead level of the lower of the following values:
3.5 micrograms or greater per deciliter, the current Maryland elevated blood lead level; or
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention blood lead reference value;
Private duty nursing services, as specified in COMAR 10.09.53.04;
Residential treatment services, as specified in COMAR 10.09.29.04; and
Therapeutic behavioral services, as specified in COMAR 10.09.34.03.

para.text: In this chapter, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined.
“Administration” means the Motor Vehicle Administration.
“Applicant” means the person requesting a certificate of title for a vehicle.
“ASED” means the Automotive Safety Enforcement Division of the Department of State Police.
“Bill of sale” means a written statement certifying the:
Ownership of something has been transferred; and
Exchange of property for an agreed sum of money or other valuable consideration.
Certificate of Title.
“Certificate of title” means a document or electronic record evidencing ownership and commonly associated with motor vehicles.
“Certificate of title” does not include a salvage certificate.
“Dealer” has the meaning stated in Transportation Article, §11-111, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Vehicle” has the meaning stated in Transportation Article, §11-176, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Vehicle identification number (VIN)” has the meaning stated in Transportation Article, §11-177, Annotated Code of Maryland.

section.heading: Used Vehicles Sold by a Dealer.

para.text: An applicant for a certificate of title to be issued for a used vehicle sold by a dealer shall provide the Administration with:
A properly assigned certificate of title or other acceptable ownership document or electronic record from the jurisdiction in which the vehicle was last registered;
A completed application for a certificate of title;
A dealer's reassignment or reassignments;
A dealer's bill of sale;
Except for §C(14) of this regulation, a Maryland safety inspection certificate; and
An odometer disclosure certification as required by COMAR 11.13.06.
Instead of a dealer's bill of sale, Maryland dealers may complete the purchase price certification on the application for a certificate of title or dealer's reassignment.
The Administration shall refuse to issue a certificate of title if:
The required documents are not furnished or the information is incomplete;
The seller's or applicant's signature is signed by an individual to whom power of attorney is granted and the writing granting the power of attorney is not furnished;
The applicant is a trust and the trust agreement naming the trust and designating the trustee or trustees is not furnished;
A transaction involves a bankruptcy and a copy of the court appointment for the bankruptcy trustee is not furnished;
A transaction involves a legal dependent and a copy of a court appointment for the legal guardian is not furnished;
An error or alteration in the documents furnished has occurred and a letter of explanation or certified statement does not clarify the error oralteration to the satisfaction of the Administration;
The lien holder to be recorded is someone other than a dealer or bona fide lending institution and a lien contract is not furnished;
The vehicle is an unrecovered stolen vehicle and a letter from the insurance company requesting issuance of a title in order to satisfy the claim is not furnished;
Certain documents are lost or omitted and the applicant fails to furnish:
A letter of indemnification which is subject to the approval of the Administration; and
Photocopies of the lost documents;
The owner or purchaser is deceased and the personal representative, legatee, distributee, legal heir, or surviving spouse has not provided:
Letters of administration/testamentary;
A legal heir form; or
A death certificate;

For a mobile home, a receipt from the Compliance Division of the Comptroller of the Treasury, which shows that the retail sales tax has been paid on a mobile home sold before January 1, 1989, is not furnished;
The vehicle is an import and the following documents are not furnished:
One of the following:
Appropriate U.S. Customs forms; or
If imported by a member of the U.S. military, Form DD788 or DD1252, and a copy of military orders or a Maryland driver's license; and
If the vehicle does not comply with U.S. Department of Transportation or U.S. Environmental Protection Agency requirements:
Receipts for conversion work; or
A bond release letter;
The vehicle was subject to a security interest and a proper termination statement is not furnished:
On a Maryland Notice of Security Interest Filing form;
On a title;
On a letter on the lien holder's letterhead; or
By electronic transmission from an approved vendor;

Except for trucks with a gross vehicle weight of 1 ton or more, truck tractors, and freight trailers, which Maryland dealers may sell without an inspection, the vehicle was sold by a Maryland dealer and the:
Vehicle's most current safety inspection is over 6 months old;
Vehicle has traveled more than 1,000 miles since the inspection certificate was issued; or
Vehicle was sold uninspected for dismantling or rebuilding and the dealer has not furnished a statement to indicate that the vehicle was sold for dismantling or rebuilding;

The ownership document is a salvage certificate from this State or another state and the inspection by a police officer in this State who is authorized to inspect salvage vehicles has not been furnished;
The applicant has requested a replacement VIN but has not:
Furnished an application for an assigned VIN; or
Had a VIN inspection completed by an authorized police officer in this State; or
The ownership document is a salvage certificate branded “Not Rebuildable — Parts Only — Not To Be Retitled”.

section.heading: Used Vehicles Sold or Transferred from Someone Other Than a Dealer.

para.text: An applicant for a certificate of title for a used vehicle transferred from someone other than a dealer shall provide the Administration with a:
Properly assigned certificate of title or other ownership document or electronic record acceptable to the Administration from the jurisdiction in which the vehicle was last registered; and
Completed application for a certificate of title.
The Administration shall refuse to issue a certificate of title if:
The required documents are not furnished or the information is incomplete;
The seller's or applicant's signature is signed by an individual to whom power of attorney is granted and the writing granting the power of attorney is not furnished;
The applicant is a trust and the trust agreement naming the trust and designating the trustee or trustees is not furnished;
A transaction involves a bankruptcy and a copy of the court appointment for the bankruptcy trustee is not furnished;
A transaction involves a legal dependent and a copy of a court appointment for the legal guardian is not furnished;
An error or alteration in the documents furnished has occurred and a letter of explanation or certified statements does not clarify the error or alteration to the satisfaction of the Administration;
The lien holder to be recorded is someone other than a dealer or bona fide lending institution and a lien contract is not furnished;
The vehicle is an unrecovered stolen vehicle and a letter from the insurance company requesting issuance of a title in order to satisfy the claim is not furnished;
Certain documents are lost or omitted and the applicant fails to furnish:
A letter of indemnification which is subject to the approval of the Administration; and
Photocopies of lost documents;
The owner or purchaser is deceased and the personal representative, legatee, distributee, legal heir, or surviving spouse has not provided as required:
Letters of administration/testamentary;
A legal heir form; or
A death certificate;

For a mobile home, a receipt from the Compliance Division of the Comptroller of the Treasury, which shows that the retail sales tax has been paid on a mobile home sold before January 1, 1989, is not furnished;
The vehicle is an import and the following documents are not furnished:
One of the following:
Appropriate U.S. Customs forms; or
If imported by a member of the U.S. military, Form DD788 or DD1252, and a copy of military orders or a Maryland driver's license; and
If the vehicle does not comply with U.S. Department of Transportation or U.S. Environmental Protection Agency requirements:
Receipts for conversion work, or
A bond release letter;
The vehicle was subject to a security interest and a proper termination statement is not furnished:
On a Maryland Notice of Security Interest Filing form;
On a title;
On a letter on the lien holder's letterhead; or
By electronic transmission from an approved vendor;

The ownership document is a salvage certificate from this State or another state and the inspection by a police officer in this State who is authorized to inspect salvage vehicles has not been furnished;
The applicant has requested a replacement VIN but has not:
Furnished an application for an assigned VIN; or
Had a VIN inspection completed by an authorized police officer in this State;
The ownership document is a salvage certificate branded “Not Rebuildable — Parts Only — Not To Be Retitled”;
The vehicle is less than 7 years old and the applicant:
Did not furnish a notarized bill of sale; and
Refuses to pay excise tax based on the greater of the purchase price or the book value of the vehicle;
The purchase price on the title is left blank and the applicant fails to produce a bill of sale;
The assignment of ownership is signed by the seller, but:
The space provided for the purchaser's name is left blank; and
A bill of sale is not furnished;
A Maryland titled vehicle is transferred as a gift between family members and the applicant fails to furnish a:
Gift certification form; and
Proof of relationship certification;
A vehicle with an open lien is transferred as a gift between a parent and child, and the transferor and transferee fail to furnish a statement signed by both parties, identifying the individual who:
Paid the down payment;
Paid the taxes;
Made all previous payments; and
Incurred the obligation for continued payment; or
The vehicle is transferred as a result of a divorce and the divorce decree is not furnished.

section.heading: Used Vehicles Transferred from Another Jurisdiction to Maryland.

para.text: An applicant for a certificate of title for a used vehicle presently titled in the applicant's name in another jurisdiction and who is now applying for a Maryland certificate of title shall provide the Administration with an:
Out-of-State title or other acceptable ownership document or electronic record from the jurisdiction where the vehicle was last registered; and
Application for a Maryland certificate of title.
The Administration shall refuse to issue a certificate of title if:
The required documents and information are not submitted or are not complete;

The out-of-State title or other acceptable ownership document or electronic record is held by a lien holder and the lien holder refuses to relinquish the title or other ownership document to the Administration;
The ownership document presented to the Administration is a:
Nonnegotiable title; or
Memorandum title;
The application for a certificate of title is signed by an individual to whom power of attorney is granted and the writing granting the power of attorney is not furnished;
The application is signed by a legal guardian and the court appointment is not furnished;

The applicant's name has changed through marriage, divorce, court order, or other method and a change of name and address notice and proof of the name change such as a marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order is not furnished;

The vehicle previously had a salvage certificate issued in this State or another state and a VIN inspection has not been completed by a police officer of this State authorized to inspect salvage vehicles;
The vehicle was previously salvaged in this State or another state and has been branded “Parts Only — Not Rebuildable”;
The vehicle is an import and the following documents are not furnished:
One of the following:
Appropriate U.S. Customs forms; or
If imported by a member of the U.S. military, Form DD788 or DD1252, and a copy of military orders or a Maryland driver's license; and
If the vehicle does not comply with U.S. Department of Transportation or U.S. Environmental Protection Agency requirements:
Receipts for conversion work, or
A bond release letter;
The applicant has requested a replacement VIN but has not:
Furnished an application for an assigned VIN number; or
Had a VIN inspection completed by an authorized police officer in this State;
An error or alteration in the documents furnished has occurred and a letter of explanation or certified statement does not clarify the error to the satisfaction of the Administration;
Certain documents are lost or omitted and the applicant fails to furnish:
A letter of indemnification subject to the approval of the Administration; and
Photocopies of lost documents; or
The ownership document is a salvage certificate branded “Not Rebuildable — Parts Only — Not to be Retitled”, or with an equivalent term.

para.text: In this chapter, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined:
“MdMUTCD” means the Maryland Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices for Streets and Highways adopted in Transportation Article, §25-104, Annotated Code of Maryland.

“Portable variable messaging sign”, also referred to as a “portable changeable message sign” in the MdMUTCD, means a sign that is capable of displaying more than one message, of which one message may be a blank display, and that is changeable manually, by remote control, or by automatic control.
“Tow truck” has the meaning stated in Transportation Article, §13-920, Annotated Code of Maryland.

section.heading: Tow Truck-mounted Portable Variable Messaging Signs.
section.text: All tow truck-mounted portable variable messaging signs shall be:

para.text: Permanently affixed to a tow truck;
Installed at a height of at least 7 feet measured from the surface of the roadway to the bottom of the sign; and
In conformance with the requirements of the MdMUTCD.
A message may be displayed only while at the scene of a crash or a disabled vehicle.
The message that may be displayed is “SLOW DOWN”, displayed as two lines in all upper-case letters.
Any tow truck-mounted portable variable messaging sign shall only display static text that:
Is colored yellow or amber, on an opaque black background;
Is one consistent letter height of at least 10 inches; and
Contains words within which the letter spacing is between 25 and 40 percent of the letter height.
The vertical spacing between the lines of text shall be between 50 and 75 percent of the letter height.
The message shall not include animation, flashing, dissolving, exploding, scrolling, or other dynamic display elements.

annotations.annotation: Transportation Article, §§ 22–218(c)(7) and 22–218.2(a), Annotated Code of Maryland
Effective date: March 16, 2026 (53:5 Md. R. 244)

codify:replace.heading: NEW TIRE FEE AND TIRE RECYCLING FEE

para.text: For purposes of the new tire fee and the tire recycling fee, the following terms have the meaning indicated.
Terms Defined.
“First sale” means a sale of a new tire that is not a sale to a wholesaler or out-of-state retailer.
“New Tire Fee” means the fee imposed under the Transportation Article, §22-421, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Tire” means a continuous rubber or similar material or synthetic material which is pneumatically designed or intended to cover or encircle a wheel which is manufactured for use on any:
Motor vehicle as defined in Tax-General Article, §9-101(g), Annotated Code of Maryland;
Trailer as defined in the Transportation Article, §11-169, Annotated Code of Maryland;
Farm implement; or
Machinery which is similar to the vehicles, trailers, or farm implements, in §B(1)(a)—(c) of this regulation.
“Tire dealer” means a person who sells new tires to:
A seller of tires in the State that is not a tire wholesaler; or
A consumer of a tire on which a recycling fee has not been paid.
“Tire recycling fee” means the fee imposed under Environment Article, §9-228, Annotated Code of Maryland.
Tire Wholesaler.
“Tire wholesaler” means a person who transfers tires to a person who is not a consumer.
“Tire wholesaler” does not mean a person who transfers a minimal number of tires on an irregular and noncontinuous basis.

A tire dealer does not pay the new tire fees or the tire recycling fees on tires sold to a tire wholesaler. If a tire dealer does not know, or cannot ascertain, whether a buyer is a tire wholesaler, the tire dealer should request a written statement from the buyer certifying that the buyer is a tire wholesaler.
Form of Certificate. A particular form for the certificate is not required, but it shall:
Set forth the buyer's name and address;
Contain the buyer's new tire fee or recycling fee account number; and
Be signed by the buyer or an authorized representative of the buyer.
A tire dealer is not required to obtain a certificate for each subsequent purchase by a tire wholesaler.

Notwithstanding the provision in Regulation .03 of this chapter, a tire dealer shall keep a certificate for a period of 4 years after the last sale upon which the tire dealer relies upon the certificate to avoid the payment of the new tire fee or the tire recycling fee.

A certificate does not relieve a tire dealer from paying new tire fees or tire recycling fees unless the certificate is accepted in good faith. A tire dealer who knows or should know that a buyer is not a tire wholesaler is required to pay the fees on tires sold to that buyer without regard to any certificate which that buyer may provide.
Buyers of tires who are not tire wholesalers do not have the option of buying tires free of new tire fees or tire recycling fees and paying the fees directly to the Comptroller.

A seller of new tires in the State is required to keep, for a period of 4 years, unless permission is obtained from the Comptroller to destroy the records after a shorter period of time, all records in connection with the sale of new tires in the State.

The records shall be adequate to distinguish a first sale of a new tire in the State by a tire dealer from all other sales of new tires in the State. This requires keeping all invoices, bills of lading, purchase orders, sales slips, cash register tapes, invoices, contracts, billing slips, journals, ledgers, inventory records, bank records, and tire wholesaler certificates upon which the dealer has relied.
A seller of new tires in the State is required to make records available for inspection and examination by the Comptroller or authorized representative at any time during business hours.

If a seller of new tires in the State fails to keep the records required, the Comptroller may compute any new tire fees or tire recycling fees which are due by using a factor that the Comptroller develops by:
A survey of the business of the seller including any available records;
A survey of other sellers of new tires engaged in the same or similar business; or
Other means.
If the seller of new tires in the State fails to keep the records required, the computation of the new tire fee and the tire recycling fee under § D of this regulation is presumptively correct.

Filing Requirements. A person who is responsible for paying the new tire fee or the tire recycling fee on tires sold during the course of a month shall file with the Comptroller a return reporting those sales, accompanied by payment of the new tire fee and the tire recycling fee, by the 21st day of the succeeding calendar month.
Filing Basis.

If the Comptroller determines from an application for a new tire fee account or tire recycling fee account, previous reporting history, or through audit that a person subject to the tire recycling fee and new tire fee is expected to remit less than $750 per month in total fees, the Comptroller may assign that person a filing basis that is less frequent than monthly.

A person assigned a filing basis which is other than monthly shall notify the Comptroller whenever that person has reason to believe that future new tire fee or tire recycling fee payment obligations will average more than $750 in total fees per month.

A person assigned a filing basis which is less frequent than four times per year, shall file with the Comptroller a supplemental return for any month, other than the month or months for which scheduled returns are due, in which fees due for that month exceed $750. This return, which is due by the due date set forth in § A of this regulation, shall also encompass any previously unreported fees accrued since the person's most recently filed return. Filing of this return does not affect a person's liability to file the next scheduled return.

The Comptroller shall make the tire fee return available to registrants, either electronically or by mail, well in advance of due dates. The failure to receive a return from the Comptroller does not, however, alter a person's obligation to file on time. A person who has not received a return from the Comptroller far enough in advance of the due date to avoid a late filing shall request an additional form from the Comptroller or file a signed schedule providing the information normally required on a return, together with the fees due, on or before the due date.
A person registered with the Comptroller for the new tire fee or the tire recycling fee shall file returns in accordance with that person's filing schedule, even if fees are not due.
Late Charges.

Statutory late charges, as provided in the Tax-General Article, § 13-601(a), 13-604, 13-701(a), and 13-708, Annotated Code of Maryland, are applicable to a person who files a return without payment in full or after the due date specified for the return.

The Comptroller is authorized to abate late charges for cause shown. The Comptroller shall consider a record of timely filing of returns as cause shown for abating late charges on one occurrence during any 12-month period, absent evidence of other noncompliance during this period. Abatements shall be made by the Comptroller without a request if qualification can be determined on an automated basis.

A person whose late charges have been waived on the basis of a previous record of timely filing is expected to take whatever steps are necessary to file subsequent returns on time. A second abatement of late charges during any 12-month period may not be authorized in the absence of a clear showing of cause relating to that return alone. The fact that late charges might have been abated on the first late return, for reasons other than a previous good filing record, is not relevant to this determination.

section.heading: Tidal Fish Licensee Intent to Fish.

para.text: Striped Bass Permit.
The total number of permits authorizing commercial licensees to fish for striped bass may not exceed:
1,231 for commercial tidal fish licensees; and
499 for fishing guide licensees.
A commercial tidal fish licensee shall possess a striped bass permit while commercially fishing for striped bass.

A commercial tidal fish licensee who has not registered to catch striped bass in accordance with §B of this regulation or who has not received a transfer of a striped bass permit and allocation in accordance with §F or G of this regulation may not catch striped bass for sale.
Registration Procedures.
Registration.
An individual may register for a permit to participate in the next quota year's striped bass fishery if they:
Are currently authorized to commercially harvest striped bass in accordance with Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland;
Have received a striped bass permit from the Department in accordance with §C of this regulation; or
Have received a permanent transfer of a striped bass permit as described in §H of this regulation.

A commercial tidal fish licensee shall register for a striped bass permit to participate in a commercial striped bass fishery in accordance with this section by September 15 of each year, or the next business day if September 15 falls on a weekend.
Any permit that is not registered by the deadline established in §B(1)(b) of this regulation shall be relinquished to the Department.
The Department shall:
Make registration available to eligible commercial tidal fish licensees not later than July 15 of each year;
Use the registrations received as of September 15 of each year, or the next business day if September 15 falls on a weekend, to determine allocations for the striped bass fishery permittees; and
Issue any striped bass permit that is not registered by the deadline established in §B(1)(b) of this regulation in accordance with §C of this regulation.
Registration Type.
When registering for a striped bass permit, a commercial tidal fish licensee shall specify and may be authorized to fish in one of the following fisheries:
Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota; or
Atlantic Ocean.

A commercial tidal fish licensee who had a striped bass permit registered in the Chesapeake Bay pound net or haul seine fisheries as of May 10, 2013 and receives shares and allocation from those permits shall register in the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery.
A commercial tidal fish licensee may not possess striped bass permits registered in both a Chesapeake Bay fishery and in the Atlantic Ocean fishery.
A striped bass permit registered in the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery as of May 10, 2013, may not be changed to a striped bass permit registered to the Atlantic Ocean fishery.
Striped Bass Permit Waiting List.

The Department shall accept an application for a striped bass permit authorizing a commercial tidal fish licensee to participate in a commercial striped bass season from any person qualified under Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland, and maintain a waiting list of commercial tidal fish license candidates to fish for striped bass.
Applicants shall be ranked and maintained in the order that their requests are received for waiting lists.
For each striped bass permit that has been revoked or voluntarily relinquished, a striped bass permit may be issued to an applicant on the waiting list.
The Department shall issue an available striped bass permit to the first person on the appropriate waiting list to fish for striped bass.
Any striped bass permit issued by the Department in accordance with this section shall be issued without an associated share of any striped bass fishery.
Commercial Fisheries.
Chesapeake Bay Individual Transferrable Quota Fishery.

In the individual transferrable quota fishery, the following gear types may be used to harvest striped bass in accordance with a tidal fish license authorization as described in Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland:
Drift gill net;
Haul seine;
Hook and line; and
Pound net.

A permittee registered in the individual transferrable quota fishery shall be provided an allocation which may be harvested at any time within the gear, time, and area restrictions set forth in COMAR 08.02.15.
Atlantic Ocean Fishery.

In the Atlantic Ocean, its coastal bays and their tidal tributaries, the following gears may be used to harvest striped bass in accordance with a tidal fish license authorization as described in Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland:
Otter and beam trawl; and
Gill net.
A permittee registered in the Atlantic Ocean fishery shall be provided an allocation which may be harvested at any time within the gear, time, and area restrictions set forth in this chapter.
Pound Net Requirements to Participate in the Striped Bass Fishery.
A pound net used to harvest striped bass shall have:
A net body or crib at least 20 feet long by 20 feet wide with a bottom panel;
Leader or hedging with a total length of at least 200 feet; and
A heart or funnel leading into the pound net crib.
A pound net used to harvest striped bass shall be registered as active with the Department in accordance with COMAR 08.02.25.07.
Temporary Transfers.
A striped bass permit may only be transferred to a valid commercial tidal fish licensee.
A properly registered striped bass permit or allocation associated with a properly registered striped bass permit may be temporarily transferred if:
The applicable fees required by Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland have been paid; and
The transfer has been completed in the manner specified by the Department in accordance with the provisions set forth in this section.
Except as provided in §G of this regulation, shares may not be transferred.
Chesapeake Bay Individual Transferrable Quota Fishery.

Prior to the start of the quota year, during the period July 15 through September 15, or the next business day if September 15 falls on a weekend, a properly registered striped bass permit for the next quota year or any part of its associated allocation may be temporarily transferred to a commercial tidal fish licensee.
During the quota year, a properly registered striped bass permit or any associated allocation may be transferred only:
During the time period established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation; and
In the increment of allocation established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation.
An individual without a properly registered striped bass permit may not receive a transfer of allocation unless the individual also receives a properly registered striped bass permit in the transfer.
A commercial tidal fish licensee may not receive an allocation transfer when the licensee possesses 2 percent or more of the total commercial quota for the Chesapeake Bay fisheries.
Atlantic Fishery.

Prior to the start of the quota year, during the period July 15 through September 15, or the next business day if September 15 falls on a weekend, a properly registered striped bass permit for the next quota year or any part of its associated allocation may be temporarily transferred to a commercial tidal fish licensee.
During the quota year, a properly registered striped bass permit or any associated allocation may be transferred only:
During the time period established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation; and
In the increment of allocation established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation.
An individual without a properly registered striped bass permit may not receive a transfer of allocation unless the individual also receives a properly registered striped bass permit in the transfer.
A commercial tidal fish licensee may not be assigned or have in possession more than four permits registered in the Atlantic Ocean and its coastal bays and tributaries.

A commercial tidal fish licensee registered in the Atlantic Ocean fishery may not receive an allocation transfer that would result in the licensee possessing more than 10 percent of the Atlantic Ocean quota.
Allocation may not be transferred between the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery and the Atlantic Ocean fishery.

For purposes of the license suspension criteria, if a commercial tidal fish licensee transfers the striped bass permit to another licensee, the transferee that committed the violation shall be held responsible.
The registration type of a striped bass permit may not be changed under a temporary transfer.
Permanent Transfers.
A properly registered striped bass permit may be permanently transferred by the owner of the permit if:
The owner makes application to the Department requesting transfer;
The transferee is a valid commercial tidal fish licensee, if the transferee is not receiving the transferor's commercial tidal fish license;
The applicable fees required by Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland have been paid; and
All striped bass permits and associated tags are returned to the Department.
The owner of a properly registered striped bass permit may permanently transfer:
A striped bass permit without transferring the owner’s tidal fish license;
The owner's share, or any portion of that share, without transferring the owner's tidal fish license or striped bass permit;
A striped bass permit and a portion of the owner's share without transferring the owner's tidal fish license, so long as the owner retains at least one striped bass permit; or
The owner's share and all striped bass permits, along with the owner's tidal fish license.

Prior to the quota year, a permanent transfer of a share or any portion of that share may only be completed from July 15 through September 15, or the next business day if September 15 falls on a weekend.
The time period described in §G(3) of this regulation does not apply to a transaction where the transferee also receives the transferor's commercial tidal fish license.
During the quota year, a permanent transfer of a share or any portion of that share may be completed only:
During the time period as established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation; and
In the increment of share as established by public notice in accordance with §H of this regulation.

A commercial tidal fish licensee registered in the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery may not receive a share in a permanent transfer that would result in the transferee possessing more than 1 percent of the total commercial quota for the Chesapeake Bay.
The transfer limit described in §G(6) of this regulation does not apply to a transaction where the transferee also receives the transferor’s commercial tidal fish license.

A commercial tidal fish licensee registered in the Atlantic Ocean fishery may not receive a share in a permanent transfer that would result in the transferee possessing more than 5 percent of the commercial quota for the Atlantic Ocean fishery.
Beneficiary Transfers.

An authorized representative of a deceased licensee may permanently transfer a striped bass permit regardless of the number of years the deceased licensee held the striped bass permit and without transferring the deceased's tidal fish license.
An authorized representative of a deceased licensee may permanently transfer a share of the striped bass fishery without transferring the deceased's tidal fish license or striped bass permit.

A transferee receiving a striped bass permit registered in the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery through a permanent transfer may not have that authorization changed to a striped bass permit registered in the Atlantic Ocean fishery.

A transferee receiving a striped bass permit registered in the Atlantic Ocean fishery through a permanent transfer may not have that authorization changed to a striped bass permit registered in the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery.
A transferee receiving a share of the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery through a permanent transfer may not transfer that share to the Atlantic Ocean fishery.
A transferee receiving a share of the Atlantic Ocean fishery through a permanent transfer may not transfer that share to the Chesapeake Bay individual transferrable quota fishery.
Modification of Transfer Conditions.

In order to implement the Atlantic States Marine Fisheries Commission Interstate Fishery Management Plan for Striped Bass, the Secretary shall establish or modify the time period for temporary or permanent transfers and the increment in which those transfers may occur by issuing a public notice on the Department's website that shall state its effective hour and date at least 48 hours in advance of the effective hour and date.
The Secretary shall make a reasonable effort to disseminate a public notice issued under this section through various other media so that an affected person has reasonable opportunity to be informed.

para.text: A person required to secure a certificate of number under 33 CFR §173.15 as amended shall submit to the Department an application for certificate of number and the required fee.
The Department shall collect the information required by 33 CFR §187 as amended.
Each certificate of number shall contain the information required by 33 CFR §174.19 as amended.
A vessel issued a Maryland certificate of number must be titled in Maryland.
Revocation.
The Department may revoke a certificate of number issued under this chapter if the vessel has been altered in such a way that it is no longer eligible to be issued a certificate of number.

Prior to revoking a certificate of number, the Department shall give the applicant notice of its intended action and an opportunity to appear at a hearing conducted in accordance with the contested case procedures set forth in State Government Article, Title 10, Subtitle 2, Annotated Code of Maryland, and COMAR 08.01.04.

section.heading: Notification to Department.
section.text: A person whose name appears as the owner of a vessel on a certificate of number shall notify the issuing authority within 15 days of:

para.text: Any address change;
The theft or recovery of the vessel;
The loss or destruction of a valid certificate of number;
The transfer of full or partial interest in the vessel;
The destruction or abandonment of the vessel;
Any email address change; or
The alteration of the vessel in such a way that it is no longer eligible to be issued a certificate of number.

section.heading: Removal of Number.
section.text: The person whose name appears on a certificate of number as the owner of a vessel shall remove the number and validation sticker from the vessel when the:

para.text: Vessel is documented by the Coast Guard;
Certificate of number is invalid under Regulation .08 of this chapter;
Vessel is no longer principally used in the state where the certificate was issued; or
Certificate of number has been revoked.
A certificate of number issued by the Department is invalid after the date when the:
Vessel is documented or required to be documented under 46 CFR §67 as amended;
Person whose name appears on the certificate of number as owner of the vessel, transfers all of his ownership of the vessel; or
Vessel is destroyed or abandoned.
A certificate of number issued by the Department is invalid if the:
Application for the certificate of number contains a false or fraudulent statement;
Fees for the issuance of the certificate of number are not paid; or
Certificate of number has been revoked.
A certificate of number is invalid 60 days after the day on which the vessel is no longer principally used in the state where the certificate was issued.
The certificate of number is invalid when the person whose name appears on the certificate involuntarily loses ownership interest in the numbered vessel by legal process.

para.text: In this subtitle, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined
“Administration” means the Maryland Cannabis Administration established under Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-201, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Advisory Council” means the Cannabis Public Health Advisory Council established under Health-General Article, §13–4502, Annotated Code of Maryland.
Agent.
“Agent” means an employee, a volunteer, or any other authorized person who acts for or at the direction of a cannabis licensee or cannabis registrant.
“Agent” includes:
Cannabis licensee agent;
Cannabis registrant agent;
Owners; and
Management companies or individuals who are employees, volunteers, or otherwise authorized by a management company.
Cannabis.

“Cannabis” means the plant cannabis sativa l. and any part of the plant, including all non-synthetically derived, extracts, cannabinoids, isomers, acids, salts, and salts of isomers, whether growing or not, with a delta–9–tetrahydrocannabinol concentration greater than 0.3 percent on a dry weight basis.
“Cannabis” includes cannabis products, seeds, seedlings, immature plants, and clones.
“Cannabis” does not include hemp or hemp products, as defined in Agriculture Article, §14–101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
Cannabis Product.
“Cannabis product” means a product that is composed of cannabis, cannabis concentrate, cannabis extract, or any other ingredient and is intended for use or consumption.
“Cannabis product” includes any product produced and regulated under this subtitle, including:
Cannabis vaporizing devices;
Concentrated cannabis products;
Edible cannabis products; and
Usable cannabis products.
Cannabis Vaporizing Device.
“Cannabis vaporizing device” means a device that can be used to deliver aerosolized or vaporized cannabis or cannabis products to an individual inhaling from the device.
“Cannabis vaporizing device” includes:
A vape pen;
Vaping liquid; and
Any component, part, or accessory of such a device regardless of whether it is sold separately, including a concentrated or infused cannabis liquid, for the purposes of heating and producing a vapor.
Canopy.
“Canopy” means the total square footage of space used by a licensee to produce flowering cannabis plants.
“Canopy” includes each layer of flowering cannabis plants grown on any rack or shelving.
“Canopy” does not include square footage used for:
Mother stock;
Propagation;
Immature or nonflowering plants;
Processing;
Drying;
Curing;
Trimming;
Storage;
Offices;
Hallways;
Pathways;
Work areas; or
Other administrative and nonproduction uses.
“Capsules” means a solid preparation containing a single serving of tetrahydrocannabinol or other cannabinoid that:
Is intended to be swallowed whole;
Not formulated to be chewable, dispersible, effervescent, orally disintegrating, used as a suspension, or consumed in a manner other than swallowed whole; and
Does not contain any added natural or artificial flavor or sweetener.
Caregiver.
“Caregiver” means an individual who has agreed to assist with a qualifying patient’s medical use of cannabis.
“Caregiver” means, for a qualifying patient younger than 18 years old:
A parent or legal guardian; and
Not more than two additional adults designated by the parent or legal guardian.

“Caregiver” does not include any designated school personnel authorized to administer medical cannabis to a student in accordance with the guidelines established under Education Article, §7-446, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Certifying provider” has the meaning stated in Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Clinical director” means an individual who:
Is appointed by a licensed dispensary to provide information on medical cannabis to qualifying patients, registered caregivers, and dispensary agents;
Meets the requirements of COMAR 14.17.04.08; and
Is registered with the Administration.
Concentrated Cannabis Product.
“Concentrated cannabis product” means a product derived from cannabis that has undergone a process to concentrate one or more active cannabinoids.
“Concentrated cannabis product” includes:
Kief;
Hashish;
Bubble hash;
Oil;
Wax;
Shatter;
Resin; or
Any other product produced by extracting cannabinoids from the plant using solvents, carbon dioxide, heat, screens, presses or steam distillation.
“Concentrated cannabis product” does not include any cannabis vaporizing device as defined in this regulation.
“Conditional license” means a temporary preapproval for a cannabis license issued pending satisfactory completion of the requirements for licensing under COMAR 14.17.05.
“Consumer” means an individual 21 years old or older who purchases cannabis or a cannabis product from a licensed dispensary or on-site consumption establishment.
Control.
“Control” means:
The decision–making authority over the management, operations, or policies that either guide a business or guide authority over the operation of the technical aspects of a business; or
Authority over the operation of the technical aspects of the business.
“Control” includes:
Holding a right to veto significant events;
The right or authority to make or veto decisions regarding operations and strategic planning, capital allocations, acquisitions, and divestments;
The right or authority to appoint or remove directors, corporate-level officers, or their equivalent;
The right or authority to make major marketing, production, and financial decisions; and
The right or authority to execute exclusive contracts or significant contracts in the aggregate of $10,000 or greater on behalf of the licensee.
“Criminal history record check” means a State and national criminal history records check in accordance with Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-505, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Data network” means the interface with the seed-to-sale tracking system that enables licensees to identify qualifying patients and registered caregivers and monitor patient allotments.
“Delivery service” means a licensee authorized to deliver cannabis in accordance with a micro license to operate a dispensary.
Dispensary.

“Dispensary” means an entity licensed under Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401, Annotated Code of Maryland, that acquires, possesses, repackages, transports, sells, distributes, or dispenses cannabis or cannabis products, including tinctures, aerosols, oils, and ointments, related supplies, and educational materials for use by qualifying patients, caregivers, or consumers through a storefront or through a delivery service, based on license type.
“Dispensary” includes standard and micro license types.
“Disproportionately impacted area” means a geographic area identified by the Office of Social Equity that has had above 150 percent of the State’s 10-year average for cannabis possession charges.
Edible Cannabis Product.
“Edible cannabis product” means a cannabis product intended for human consumption by oral ingestion, in whole or in part.
“Edible cannabis product” includes a cannabis product that dissolves or disintegrates in the mouth.
“Edible cannabis product” does not include any concentrated cannabis products, infused non-edible cannabis products, or capsules or tinctures that do not contain any food or food ingredients.

“Electronic manifest” means the comprehensive report created by a licensee within the Administration’s designated seed-to-sale tracking system to record the chain of custody of a cannabis transfer or delivery.
“Green waste” means unauthorized, misbranded, contaminated, unused, surplus, returned, or out-of-date cannabis or product containing cannabis.
Grower.

“Grower” means an entity licensed under Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401, Annotated Code of Maryland, that cultivates or packages cannabis and is authorized by the Administration to provide cannabis to other licensees and registered independent testing laboratories.
“Grower” includes standard and micro license types.
High Potency Product.

“High potency product” means a product that exceeds the maximum potency for consumers, as established by the Administration, and may only be sold or distributed to a qualifying patient or registered caregiver.
“High potency product” includes:
A concentrated cannabis product that exceeds 1 gram in total weight; and
An edible cannabis product, capsule, or tincture that exceeds 10 milligrams THC per serving and 100 milligrams THC per package.
Home Cultivation Product.
“Home cultivation product” means the clones, seeds, seedlings, stalks, roots, and stems of the cannabis plant for home cultivation.
“Home cultivation product” does not include any plant that is:
Wider than 8 inches;
Taller than 8 inches; or
Otherwise in a vegetative or flowering state.
“Incubator space” means a facility where a micro licensee may operate in accordance with Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-406, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Independent testing laboratory” means a facility, entity, or site that is:
Registered with the Administration to perform tests on cannabis or cannabis products;
Independent of any entity licensed under Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401, Annotated Code of Maryland, to grow, process or dispense cannabis; and
Accredited as operating to International Organization for Standardization (ISO) standard 17025 by an accreditation body:
Operating in accordance with ISO standard ISO/IEC 17011; and
That is a signatory to the International Laboratory Accreditation Cooperation (ILAC) Mutual Recognition Arrangement (MRA).

“Infused non-edible cannabis product” means ointment, salve, suppository, dermal patch, cartridge, or any other product containing cannabis that has been processed so that the dried leaves and flowers are integrated into other material that is not intended for human consumption by inhalation or oral ingestion.

“Law enforcement agency” means a governmental police force, sheriff’s office, security force, or law enforcement organization of the State, a county, or a municipal corporation that by statute, ordinance, or common law is authorized to enforce the general criminal laws of the State.
Licensee.
“Licensee” means a business licensed by the Administration in accordance with Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Licensee” includes any:
Micro grower;
Micro processor;
Micro dispensary;
Standard grower;
Standard processor;
Standard dispensary;
Incubator space; and
On-site consumption establishment.
Liquid Edible Product.
“Liquid edible product” means an edible cannabis product that is a liquid beverage or liquid food-based product for which the intended use is oral consumption.
“Liquid edible product” excludes a tincture as defined in this section.
“Micro license” means a license to operate a cannabis business that does not exceed the limits established in Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401(c)(2), Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Neutral age screen” means a mechanism to verify a user's age by requiring them to enter their date of birth to gain access to areas of a website that are not designed for children.
“On-site consumption establishment” means an entity licensed by the Administration to distribute cannabis or cannabis products for on-site consumption other than by smoking indoors.
“Operational” means physically and actively engaged in the cultivation, processing, or dispensing of cannabis.
“Owner” means a person with an ownership interest in a cannabis license.
“Ownership interest” means a direct or indirect equity interest in a cannabis license, including in its shares or stock.
“Passive investor” means an individual or an entity that:
Holds an aggregate ownership interest of less than 5 percent in a cannabis licensee; and
Does not have control of the cannabis licensee.
“Personal use amount” means an amount of:
Cannabis that does not exceed 1.5 ounces;
Concentrated cannabis that does not exceed 12 grams; or
Cannabis products containing no more than 750 milligrams of delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol.

“Principal officer” means a board member, a president, a vice president, a secretary, a treasurer, a partner, an officer, a managing member, or any other individual with a profit sharing, financial interest, or revenue sharing arrangement, including an individual with the authority to control a cannabis license.
“Processing” means the manufacture of usable cannabis into a cannabis concentrate or manufacture of a cannabis-infused product.
Processor.
“Processor” means an entity licensed by the Administration in accordance with Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401, Annotated Code of Maryland, that:
Transforms cannabis into another product, or an extract, and packages and labels the cannabis product; and
Is authorized by the Administration to provide cannabis to cannabis licensees and registered independent testing laboratories.
“Processor” includes standard and micro license types.
“Qualifying patient” means an individual who:
Has been provided with a valid written certification by an Administration-registered certifying provider in accordance with a bona fide provider–patient relationship; and
If younger than 18 years old, has a caregiver.
Registrant.
“Registrant” means a business registered by the Administration to operate in the cannabis industry.
“Registrant” includes:
Independent testing laboratory;
Transporter business;
Security guard company; and
Waste disposal company.
Residence.
“Residence” means a house, condominium, apartment, or any other dwelling unit under legal possession of an individual.
“Residence” does not mean a dormitory or other on-campus college or university housing.

“Seed-to-sale tracking system” means a software system procured by the Administration that tracks cannabis from either the seed or immature plant stage, until the cannabis is sold to a patient, caregiver, or consumer.

“Serious adverse event” means an undesirable experience associated with the use of cannabis where the outcome was death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability or permanent damage, congenital anomaly or birth defect or any other important medical event.
“Social equity applicant” means an applicant for a cannabis license or cannabis registration that:
Has at least 65 percent ownership and control held by one or more individuals who:
Have lived in a disproportionately impacted area for at least 5 of the 10 years immediately preceding the submission of the application;
Attended a public school in a disproportionately impacted area for at least 5 years; or

For at least 2 years, attended a 4-year institution of higher education in the State where at least 40 percent of the individuals who attend the institution of higher education are eligible for a Pell Grant; or
Meets any other criteria established by the Administration.
Social Equity Licensee.
“Social equity licensee” means a social equity applicant who has been awarded a cannabis license.
“Social equity licensee” includes a grower, processor, or dispensary that:
Held a Stage One Preapproval for a license before October 1, 2022; and
Was not operational before October 1, 2022.
“Social Equity Partnership Grant” means a program within the Office of Social Equity that awards grants to promote qualifying partnerships between operational licensees and social equity licensees.
“Standard license” means a license to operate a cannabis business that complies with the limits established in Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-401(c)(1), Annotated Code of Maryland.
“State Cannabis Testing Laboratory” means the laboratory operated by the Administration in accordance with Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-204, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Tetrahydrocannabinol” or “THC”, unless otherwise specified, means any:
Tetrahydrocannabinol, including delta-8-tetrahydrocannabinol, delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol, and delta-10-tetrahydrocannabinol, regardless of how derived;
Other cannabinoid, other than cannabidiol that the Administration determines to cause intoxication; and
Other chemically similar compound, substance, derivative, or isomer of tetrahydrocannabinol, as identified by the Administration.
“Tincture” means a solution that is:
Dissolved in alcohol, glycerin, or vegetable oil; and
Distributed in a dropper bottle of 4 ounces or less.
Usable Cannabis.
“Usable cannabis” means the dried leaves and flowers of the cannabis plant.

“Usable cannabis” does not include seedlings, seeds, stems, stalks, or roots of the plant or the weight of any noncannabis ingredients combined with cannabis, such as ingredients added to prepare a topical administration.
Usable Cannabis Product.
“Usable cannabis product” means a prepackaged product containing usable cannabis.
“Usable cannabis product” includes:
A pre-rolled amount of usable cannabis;
Securely stored, sealed, and labeled amount of usable cannabis; and
Any other type or amount of usable cannabis that has been wrapped, rolled, or otherwise encased for the purposes of smoking.
Visitor.
“Visitor” means a guest at a licensed or registered premises who is not a registered agent employed by the licensee or registrant.
“Visitor” does not include:

A qualifying patient, registered caregiver, adult-use consumer, or any children who accompany a qualifying patient or registered caregiver for the sole purpose of purchasing cannabis or cannabis products; or
An Administration investigator.
“Written certification” means a certification that:
Is issued by a certifying provider to a qualifying patient with whom the provider has a bona fide provider–patient relationship;

Includes a written statement certifying that, in the certifying provider’s professional opinion, after having completed an assessment of the patient’s medical history and current medical condition, the patient has a condition:
That meets the inclusion criteria and does not meet the exclusion criteria of the certifying provider’s application; and
For which the potential benefits of the medical use of cannabis would likely outweigh the health risks for the patient; and

May include a written statement certifying that, in the certifying provider’s professional opinion, a 30–day supply of medical cannabis would be inadequate to meet the medical needs of the qualifying patient.

section.heading: Technical Authority.
section.text: The Maryland Cannabis Administration’s Technical Authority for Cannabis Testing (Effective February 2025), or Technical Authority, is incorporated by reference.

section.heading: Pilot Authority.
section.text: The Administration may require licensees to participate in pilot programs to test efficacy of proposed initiatives to improve regulation of cannabis, provided the following criteria are met.

para.text: Pilot participants shall be selected according to objective criteria as determined by the Administration.
No documents produced by a licensee as part of the pilot program may be used to gather a compliance case against a licensee.
The Administration shall provide a minimum of 60 days’ notice to any licensee selected to participate prior to the start date of pilot participation.
The Administration shall maintain strict confidentiality and data protection standards for all data collected, created, or maintained under any pilot program. Such standards shall include:
Restricting data collection and use to the explicit, legitimate purposes of the pilot program; and
Using appropriate encryption, access controls, and other measures to prevent unauthorized disclosure or misuse.
The Administration may consider a licensee's written request to opt out of a pilot program based on:
Anticipated hardship by the proposed pilot program; or
Participation in an Administration pilot program in the prior 6 months.

section.heading: Issuance of a License or Rescission of a Conditional License.

para.text: Conditional License Period.
The conditional license period:
Begins on the day that a conditional license is issued to the selected applicant; and
Expires 24 months after the day that conditional license was issued or at the end of an extension granted by the Administration.
A conditional licensee may apply for an extension of the conditional license.

The Administration may approve a one-time extension of up to 6 months, if the Administration determines the conditional licensee has made consistent good faith efforts to establish a cannabis business.
For purposes of determining consistent good faith effort in §A(3) of this regulation, the Administration:
Will consider demonstrating legal control of a premises to indicate consistent good faith effort if the premises:
Is suitable for the operations of the cannabis license; and
Complies with local planning and zoning requirements; and

May use its discretion to determine that a conditional licensee has demonstrated consistent good faith effort if the conditional licensee provides adequate documentation that a political subdivision has unduly burdened its efforts toward becoming operational, as defined in Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, §36-405, Annotated Code of Maryland.
During the conditional license period, a conditional licensee shall:
Complete a supplemental license application;
Establish legal control of the proposed site, through lease, purchase, or other means, for the cannabis business;
Notify the Administration of establishment of legal control of the proposed site;
Within 6 months of being issued a conditional license, demonstrate adequate capitalization to enable the business to become operational;
Gain zoning or planning approval from a political subdivision, if applicable; and
Register the business with the State Department of Assessment and Taxation.
During a conditional license period, a conditional licensee may not:
Engage in purchasing, possessing, cultivating, manufacturing, or selling cannabis or cannabis products;
Make any transfer of an ownership interest that causes a change in the individual or entity that holds the controlling ownership interest;
Make any transfer of control, as defined in COMAR 14.17.01; and

If the conditional licensee qualified as a social equity applicant, make any transfer of an ownership interest that causes the conditional licensee to no longer comply with the social equity applicant definition in COMAR 14.17.01.
During a conditional license period, a conditional licensee may obtain additional resources by adding:
Grants and loans from new or existing financial sources not listed in the initial application; and
Owners and passive investors.

Any additional resources obtained by a conditional licensee made under §A(7) of this regulation may not violate this subtitle or Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, Title 36, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland.
Rescission of a Conditional License. The Administration may rescind a conditional license if a conditional licensee, or any individual or entity included in the supplemental license application:
Has been convicted of or pleaded nolo contendere to a crime involving moral turpitude, whether or not any appeal or other proceeding is pending to have conviction or plea set aside;
Fraudulently or deceptively attempts to obtain a license;
Is ineligible to hold an ownership interest in or control of a business licensed under Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, Title 36, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland;
Fails to reveal any material fact pertaining to the conditional licensee’s qualification for a license;
Fails to submit a complete supplemental license application;
Fails to become licensed and operational within:
24 months after the day the conditional license was issued; or
If granted an extension, the day after the expiration of any extension granted by the Administration;
Fails to comply with §A(5) and (6) of this regulation;
Is not registered or in good standing with the State Department of Assessment and Taxation; or
Has taxes in arrears in any jurisdiction.
The Administration may award a license on a determination that:
The conditional licensee has submitted a complete supplemental license application;
The supplemental license application, including any individual or entity included in the application, does not violate §B of this regulation;
The license fee specified in COMAR 14.17.21 has been paid;
All inspections are passed, and all the conditional licensee’s operations conform to the specifications of the application as approved pursuant to this chapter; and
The proposed premises:
Are under the legal control of the conditional licensee; and
Comply with all zoning and planning requirements.
A licensed business may not grow, process, distribute, dispense, or otherwise begin business operations without approval of written documentation under §C of this regulation by the Administration.
The Administration may not award a license to a business who has registered with the State Department of Assessment and Taxation using a legal name that:
Uses the terms “cannabis”, “marijuana”, or other synonym related to controlled substances;
Suggests the use of cannabis as an intoxicant; or
Incorporates any copyrighted material or trademark or service mark attributable to another entity.
The Administration may suspend, fine, restrict, or revoke a license, including a conditional license, if:
A licensee has fraudulently or deceptively submitted written documentation to the Administration;
A licensee has violated Alcoholic Beverages and Cannabis Article, Title 36, Subtitle 11, Annotated Code of Maryland;
A licensee began operations prior to Administration approval; or

It is determined that a licensee has not complied with statements in the application, including statements about standards of operation or employment practices related to diversity, equity, and inclusion.
